@@ -95,7 +95,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-azw","title":"Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests ## Context Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i) ## What to Do Create functional tests for databases and cloud CLIs: ### PostgreSQL Tests - check_postgres_connection(): psql -c "SELECT 1" - check_postgres_role(): Verify ubuntu role exists ### Cloud CLI Tests - check_vault_configured(): VAULT_ADDR set (info status if not) - check_wrangler_auth(): wrangler whoami - check_supabase_auth(): Access token exists and not expired - check_vercel_auth(): vercel whoami ### Status Codes - 0: Pass - 1: Fail (with fix suggestion) - 2: Skip (not installed) - 3: Info (not configured, but optional) ## Acceptance Criteria - Each cloud CLI has auth check - PostgreSQL connection and role verified - Status codes used consistently - Timeout on external calls (prevent hangs) ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add cloud/db check functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:20.261596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:59:50.118089-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:59:50.118089-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented check_postgres_connection(), check_postgres_role(), and enhanced cloud CLI checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-azw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:35.134842-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b1yk","title":"FEATURE: Installer UX Improvements","description":"## Parent Epic agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0) ## Summary Improve the user experience during the 10-15 minute installation process. Currently, users see scrolling text with no clear indication of progress or expected duration. This causes anxiety and premature terminal closing. ## Background & Motivation The UX audit noted: > "15 minutes of waiting with scrolling text is scary... User thought: > 'Is it stuck? Should I do something? When will this end?'" Users who close their terminal during installation cause failed setups and support burden. Clear progress indicators reduce anxiety and improve completion rates. ## Components ### 1. Progress Header (Always Visible) Add a persistent header that shows: \`\`\` ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Agent Flywheel Installer v0.1.0 ║ ║ Progress: [████████████░░░░░░░░] 60% (8 of 13 modules) ║ ║ Current: Installing programming languages... ║ ║ Elapsed: 5m 23s | Remaining: ~3-4 min ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ \`\`\` This should update in-place (using \\r or terminal control codes). ### 2. Module-Level Progress Each module shows: - [1/13] Setting up user account... - [2/13] Installing shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh)... - [3/13] Installing programming languages... - etc. ### 3. Time Estimates Based on benchmarks: - User setup: ~30 seconds - Shell: ~60 seconds - Languages (bun, uv, rust, go): ~180 seconds - Dev tools: ~120 seconds - Cloud CLIs: ~90 seconds - Agents: ~60 seconds - Stack tools: ~120 seconds - Database: ~180 seconds (if enabled) - Total: ~12-15 minutes Show "Remaining: ~X min" based on current progress. ### 4. Clear Completion Signal When done: \`\`\` ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ ✓ Installation Complete! ║ ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣ ║ ║ ║ Total time: 12m 34s ║ ║ Modules installed: 13/13 ║ ║ ║ ║ NEXT STEPS: ║ ║ 1. Type 'exit' to disconnect ║ ║ 2. Reconnect with: ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP ║ ║ 3. Start coding with 'cc' (Claude Code) ║ ║ ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ \`\`\` ### 5. Troubleshooting Guidance If a step fails: \`\`\` ⚠ Warning: Module 'install_rust' had issues Common causes: • Network timeout - will retry automatically • Package mirror busy - usually resolves on retry The installer will continue with other modules. You can run the installer again to retry failed modules. \`\`\` ## Technical Implementation ### Progress Tracking The installer already has phases/modules. We need to: 1. Count total modules at start 2. Track current module number 3. Calculate elapsed time 4. Estimate remaining based on benchmarks \`\`\`bash # Global tracking TOTAL_MODULES=13 CURRENT_MODULE=0 START_TIME=$(date +%s) # Progress function show_progress() { local current=$1 local name=$2 local percent=$((current * 100 / TOTAL_MODULES)) local elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME)) local remaining=$((elapsed * (TOTAL_MODULES - current) / current)) 2>/dev/null || 0 # Clear line and print progress printf "\\r[%d/%d] %3d%% | Elapsed: %dm %ds | Remaining: ~%dm | %s" \\ "$current" "$TOTAL_MODULES" "$percent" \\ $((elapsed/60)) $((elapsed%60)) \\ $((remaining/60)) "$name" } \`\`\` ### Terminal Compatibility - Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates - Fall back to simple output if terminal doesn't support - Handle both interactive and non-interactive modes ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Progress percentage visible throughout installation - [ ] Current module name displayed - [ ] Elapsed time shown - [ ] Estimated remaining time shown - [ ] Clear completion message with next steps - [ ] Failed module guidance (don't panic) - [ ] Works in both SSH terminal and console - [ ] No breaking changes to existing installer logic ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration - Should integrate progress for key setup step too ## Testing Plan 1. Run full installation and verify progress updates 2. Test with slow network (progress should still work) 3. Test with failed module (guidance should appear) 4. Test in different terminals (iTerm, Terminal.app, Windows Terminal) 5. Test via tmux (should still work) ## Future Enhancements - Web-based progress dashboard (installer pings API) - Sound notification on completion - Desktop notification via terminal hooks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:52.137253-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:34:51.998446-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:34:51.998446-05:00","close_reason":"Design specification complete. Detailed progress display requirements, terminal compatibility notes, and time estimation logic documented. Closing to unblock implementation task 3u3m.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b1yk","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:20.725081-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b9v","title":"Task: Update Step 12 with Learning Hub integration","description":"# Update Step 12 with Learning Hub Integration ## Parent Feature Wizard Integration (mna) ## Goal Update the wizard's final step (launch-onboarding/page.tsx) to prominently feature the new Learning Hub as the primary continuation path. ## Current State Step 12 currently: 1. Shows celebration confetti 2. Has "Start the onboarding tutorial" card with `onboard` command 3. Shows "What you can do now" quick reference 4. Has "Continue to Part Two: The Workflow" link ## Proposed Changes ### Update the Main CTA Replace the command-focused card with a Learning Hub card: Before: ```tsx

Start the onboarding tutorial

Learn the basics with an interactive tutorial:

 ``` After: ```tsx

Continue Your Learning Journey

Master your new environment with 9 guided lessons:

{/* Primary CTA */} {/* Secondary option */}
Prefer the terminal? Run onboard
``` ### Update "Part Two" Messaging The card that links to `/workflow` should mention the Learning Hub: ```tsx

Ready for the Advanced Workflow?

After completing the Learning Hub basics, dive into the powerful multi-agent workflow that lets you build production-ready software at incredible speed.

``` ### Visual Hierarchy 1. **Celebration header** - "Congratulations!" (keep as-is) 2. **Learning Hub CTA** - Primary call-to-action (NEW) 3. **Quick reference cards** - "What you can do now" (keep as-is) 4. **Part Two card** - Advanced workflow (update messaging) ### Analytics Update Track clicks to Learning Hub: ```typescript const { trackEvent } = useWizardAnalytics(); // In the Learning Hub button onClick trackEvent('learning_hub_started', { from: 'step_12' }); ``` ## File Changes - Update: `apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Learning Hub is the primary CTA - [ ] CLI option still available for terminal users - [ ] Analytics tracks Learning Hub clicks - [ ] Visual hierarchy emphasizes learning journey - [ ] Messaging is clear about the two paths (Learn Hub vs. Advanced) ## Notes - Don't remove the `onboard` command option - some users prefer CLI - Keep the celebration/confetti - it's a nice touch - Consider adding a progress indicator preview showing 0/9 lessons","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:23:18.475445-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:29:33.607249-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:29:33.607249-05:00","close_reason":"Added trackConversion analytics for Learning Hub clicks on Step 12. Removed unused CommandCard import.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b9v","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bri","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:11.18954-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bcin","title":"TASK: Add 'Getting Back In' reconnection section to launch-onboarding","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz (Post-Installation Onboarding Experience) ## Summary Add a prominent "Getting Back In" section to launch-onboarding that explains how to reconnect to the VPS after closing the terminal, and how to set up SSH config for easier access. ## Rationale The UX audit identified reconnection as a critical gap: > "If users close their laptop, they don't know how to get back to their work" Users who close their terminal and can't get back in will: - Feel frustrated and stuck - Potentially abandon the project - Flood support with "how do I get back in?" questions ## Content Specification ### Section: "Getting Back In" Place this AFTER "Your First 5 Minutes" but BEFORE existing quick reference cards. \`\`\`tsx

Getting Back In

Closed your terminal? Here's how to reconnect:

{/* Step 1: Open terminal */}

1. Open your terminal app

Ghostty, WezTerm, or Windows Terminal

{/* Step 2: SSH command */}

2. Connect to your VPS

{/* Step 3: Resume session */}

3. Resume your session (if using NTM)

This brings back exactly where you left off - including any running Claude sessions!

{/* SSH Config tip */}
💡 Pro tip: Set up SSH config for easier access

Add this to your local ~/.ssh/config file: 

{
\`Host myserver
    HostName \${vpsIP}
    User ubuntu
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519\`
}

Then just type: ssh myserver

\`\`\` ### Visual Design - Blue-tinted card (different from other sections) - Terminal icon for visual recognition - Numbered steps - SSH config in collapsible details ### Important Notes - Use the user's actual IP from localStorage - Show both Mac and Windows commands where they differ - Keep it above the fold if possible (it's important!) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] "Getting Back In" section exists and is prominent - [ ] Three clear steps: open terminal, SSH, resume session - [ ] SSH command includes user's IP - [ ] NTM commands explained - [ ] SSH config tip in collapsible section - [ ] Works for both Mac and Windows - [ ] Mobile-friendly ## Dependencies - TASK: Add 'Your First 5 Minutes' section (comes before this in the page) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.828607-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:44:12.539227-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bcin","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:22:22.834242-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bfv","title":"Add tailscale module to acfs.manifest.yaml","description":"## Task Add Tailscale as a module in the ACFS manifest. ## Module Definition ```yaml tailscale: name: Tailscale description: Zero-config mesh VPN for secure remote access category: networking # New category or under 'tools' phase: cli_tools # Install early, before agents enabled_by_default: true install: method: script script: | # Add Tailscale apt repository curl -fsSL https://pkgs.tailscale.com/stable/ubuntu/$(lsb_release -cs).noarmor.gpg \ | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/tailscale-archive-keyring.gpg >/dev/null echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/tailscale-archive-keyring.gpg] \ https://pkgs.tailscale.com/stable/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) main" \ | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/tailscale.list sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y tailscale sudo systemctl enable tailscaled verify: command: tailscale version expected_pattern: "^tailscale" post_install_message: | Tailscale installed! To connect your VPS to your Tailscale network: sudo tailscale up Then log in with your Google account at the URL shown. docs_url: https://tailscale.com/kb/ tags: - networking - vpn - security - google-sso ``` ## Considerations 1. **Ubuntu version handling**: Use `$(lsb_release -cs)` for codename 2. **Fallback for older Ubuntu**: Tailscale supports back to Ubuntu 18.04 3. **Systemd enablement**: Ensure tailscaled starts on boot 4. **Skip if already installed**: Check before re-installing ## Verification After this task: - `acfs --list-modules` shows tailscale - `acfs --print-plan` includes tailscale in cli_tools phase","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:57:40.857933-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:32:04.587508-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:32:04.587508-05:00","close_reason":"Added network.tailscale module to manifest. Generator creates install_network.sh. Module is Phase 5, enabled by default, uses apt repository method."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bq3","title":"Ubuntu Auto-Upgrade Feature","description":"# Ubuntu Auto-Upgrade Feature ## Overview Automatically detect Ubuntu version and upgrade to the latest stable release (25.10) before running the main ACFS installation. The entire process should be **fully automated and non-interactive**. ## Problem Statement Many cloud providers (OVH, Contabo, Hetzner) default to older Ubuntu versions (22.04, 24.04). ACFS works best on the latest Ubuntu, and some packages may not be available on older versions. Currently, ACFS just warns about old versions but doesn't help upgrade. ## Solution Add a pre-installation phase that: 1. Detects current Ubuntu version 2. Calculates upgrade path to target version 3. Automatically performs sequential do-release-upgrade operations 4. Handles reboots with automatic resume via systemd service 5. Continues ACFS installation after reaching target version ## Design Philosophy - **Fully automated**: No user interaction required once started - **Resilient**: Handle reboots, network issues, and failures gracefully - **Observable**: Comprehensive logging for debugging - **Opt-out available**: Users can skip with --skip-ubuntu-upgrade ## Default Behavior With the standard one-liner: ```bash curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe ``` The installer will: 1. Detect Ubuntu version 2. If below 25.10, automatically upgrade (no prompts) 3. Reboot as needed 4. Resume and continue upgrading until at target 5. Proceed with ACFS installation ## Opt-out ```bash curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe --skip-ubuntu-upgrade ``` ## Technical Constraints 1. Ubuntu only allows upgrading to NEXT version (no skipping) - 22.04 → 24.04 (LTS-to-LTS is special case) - 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 (sequential) 2. Each upgrade requires a reboot 3. do-release-upgrade takes 30-60 minutes per version 4. SSH sessions will disconnect during reboot (users must reconnect) 5. Need systemd service for automatic resume after reboot ## Success Criteria - [ ] Detects Ubuntu version accurately - [ ] Calculates correct upgrade path - [ ] Performs upgrade non-interactively - [ ] Creates systemd service for post-reboot resume - [ ] Chains multiple upgrades without manual intervention - [ ] Resumes ACFS installation after final upgrade - [ ] Cleans up systemd service after completion - [ ] Handles errors with clear logging - [ ] Works across SSH disconnection/reconnection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:18:52.892435-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:17:59.471452-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:17:59.471452-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: ubu.1-ubu.12 implemented the upgrade system, ubu.8 (d7c) documented it. Feature is fully implemented with version detection, path calculation, systemd resume, error recovery, and documentation."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bri","title":"Task: Create learning hub dashboard page","description":"# Create Learning Hub Dashboard Page ## Parent Feature Learning Hub Core Infrastructure (tor) ## Goal Create the `/learn` page that serves as the entry point and dashboard for the learning section. ## Design ### Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Learning Hub │ │ Your journey to mastering agentic development │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Progress: 4/9 lessons complete ████████░░░░░ 44% │ │ │ │ [Continue: Lesson 5 - NTM Command Center →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ │ │ │ ✓ │ │ ✓ │ │ ✓ │ │ │ │Welcome │ │Linux │ │SSH │ ... (9 cards) │ │ │5 min │ │8 min │ │6 min │ │ │ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ │ │ │ │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ Quick Reference │ │ [Commands] [Tools] [Glossary] │ │ │ │ Already set up? [Continue to Advanced Workflow →] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Components Needed #### 1. Progress Section - Completion percentage with animated progress bar - "Continue where you left off" CTA with next lesson - Shows 0% state for new users #### 2. Lesson Grid - 3x3 grid of lesson cards (responsive: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop) - Each card shows: - Completion status icon (✓ complete, ● current, ○ pending) - Lesson title - Duration estimate - Brief description on hover/expand #### 3. Quick Reference Section - Links to command reference, tools, glossary - Lower priority (can be added later) #### 4. Advanced Users Section - Link to `/workflow` for users who want to skip basics - "Already know the basics?" messaging ### Implementation ```typescript // app/learn/page.tsx "use client"; import { useCompletedLessons, LESSONS, getNextLesson } from "@/lib/lessonProgress"; import { LessonCard } from "@/components/lesson-card"; import { ProgressBar } from "@/components/ui/progress"; export default function LearnPage() { const [completed, markComplete] = useCompletedLessons(); const next = getNextLesson(); const progress = (completed.length / LESSONS.length) * 100; return (
{/* Header */}

Learning Hub

Your journey to mastering agentic development

{/* Progress Card */}
Progress: {completed.length}/{LESSONS.length} lessons {Math.round(progress)}%
{next && ( )} {/* Lesson Grid */}
{LESSONS.map(lesson => ( ))}
); } ``` ## Component: LessonCard Create reusable card component at `components/lesson-card.tsx`: - Links to `/learn/[slug]` - Shows status, title, duration - Hover state with description ## Styling - Use existing Card component from shadcn/ui - Match color scheme from wizard (oklch colors) - Progress bar animation ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page renders at `/learn` - [ ] Progress bar shows correct percentage - [ ] All 9 lesson cards display - [ ] Cards link to correct lesson pages - [ ] "Continue" button shows next uncompleted lesson - [ ] Responsive on mobile/tablet/desktop - [ ] Consistent styling with rest of app ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/page.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:19:31.852006-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.737052-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.737052-05:00","close_reason":"Dashboard page complete at /learn - has progress section with circular progress, all 9 lesson cards, continue button, quick reference links, and responsive grid layout.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bri","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:07.112276-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bri","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.031729-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
